Column descriptions:
  cross|DataFlow|RecordRegistry: CHAR(10) primary key uniquely identifying each data flow record in the DataFlow table.
  cross|DataFlow|FlowStamp: TIMESTAMP(6) for the moment this data flow record was created or logged. Microsecond precision.
  cross|DataFlow|FlowTag: A short identifier or tag (VARCHAR(20)) for the data flow, previously 'DataFlowID'.
  cross|DataFlow|OrigNation: VARCHAR(80) storing the source/origin country's name for this data flow.
  cross|DataFlow|DestNation: VARCHAR(80) storing the destination country's name for this data flow.
  cross|DataFlow|OrigActor: VARCHAR(150) referencing the source entity/system initiating the flow (person, organization, or app).
  cross|DataFlow|DestActor: VARCHAR(150) referencing the destination entity/system receiving the flow.
  cross|DataFlow|ChanProto: VARCHAR(30) for the protocol used (HTTP, FTP, SFTP, etc.). Currently possible values: (Blockchain, SFTP, Private Network, HTTPS).
  cross|DataFlow|ChanFreq: VARCHAR(25) specifying how frequently data is transferred (daily, weekly, on-demand, etc.). Currently possible values: (Weekly, Hourly, Real-time, Daily).
  cross|DataFlow|DataSizeMB: NUMERIC(12,2) representing the size in MB of each data transfer.
  cross|DataFlow|DurMin: SMALLINT capturing the average or actual duration (in minutes) of the data flow.
  cross|DataFlow|BwidthPct: NUMERIC(5,2) for bandwidth utilization percentage (0 to 100%).
  cross|DataFlow|SuccessPct: NUMERIC(5,2) measuring the success rate (0–100%) of the flow attempts.
  cross|DataFlow|ErrTally: SMALLINT counting the total errors or failures observed for this data flow.
  cross|DataFlow|RtryTally: SMALLINT storing how many retry attempts were made after failures.
  cross|RiskManagement|RiskTrace: SERIAL primary key uniquely identifying each risk management record.
  cross|RiskManagement|FlowLink: CHAR(10) referencing DataFlow(RecordRegistry), linking this risk record to a specific data flow.
  cross|RiskManagement|RecordRegistry: CHAR(10) formerly 'RecordID'; can be used for cross-references or historical reasons.
  cross|RiskManagement|RiskAssess: NUMERIC(4,2) storing the assessed risk score (0–100 or custom).
  cross|RiskManagement|RiskMitState: VARCHAR(40) describing how the risk is being mitigated. Currently possible values: (Pending, Partial, Implemented).
  cross|RiskManagement|SecureAction: TEXT describing security measures or controls used to reduce identified risks. Currently possible values: (Adequate, Strong, Insufficient).
  cross|RiskManagement|BreachNotify: TEXT outlining the procedure for breach notification if a security event occurs. Currently possible values: (Partial, Established, Missing).
  cross|RiskManagement|IncidentPlan: TEXT capturing the incident response plan or steps for handling security/operational events. Currently possible values: (Missing, Active, Outdated).
  cross|RiskManagement|IncidentCount: SMALLINT counting incidents recorded for this data flow or scenario.
  cross|RiskManagement|BreachCount: SMALLINT counting data breaches recorded.
  cross|RiskManagement|NearMissNum: SMALLINT enumerating near misses (close calls that didn't escalate).
  cross|RiskManagement|AvgResolHrs: NUMERIC(5,2) average incident resolution time in hours.
  cross|RiskManagement|SLApct: NUMERIC(4,2) indicating SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance percentage (0–100%).
  cross|RiskManagement|CostUSD: NUMERIC(11,2) capturing the cost of compliance in USD (security, risk measures, etc.).
  cross|RiskManagement|PenUSD: NUMERIC(11,2) storing potential or actual penalty/fine risk in USD.
  cross|RiskManagement|CoverageState: VARCHAR(40) describing insurance coverage status (e.g., 'Full', 'Partial', 'None'). Currently possible values for discovered data: (Limited, Adequate).
  cross|RiskManagement|ResidRiskLevel: VARCHAR(40) naming the residual risk level after mitigation (Low, Medium, High, etc.). Currently possible values: (Medium, High, Low).
  cross|RiskManagement|CtrlEff: NUMERIC(4,2) rating the effectiveness of current controls (0–100).
  cross|RiskManagement|CompScore: NUMERIC(4,2) measuring overall compliance posture (0–100 or custom).
  cross|RiskManagement|MaturityLevel: VARCHAR(40) describing the maturity of processes (Initial, Managed, Optimized, etc.). Currently possible values: (Optimized, Managed, Initial).
  cross|RiskManagement|NextRevDate: DATE specifying when the next risk/compliance review is scheduled.
  cross|RiskManagement|PlanState: VARCHAR(45) capturing improvement or remediation plan status (e.g., 'Planned', 'Completed'). Currently possible values: (On Track, Not Started, Delayed).
  cross|DataProfile|ProfileTrace: SERIAL primary key identifying each data profile record.
  cross|DataProfile|FlowSign: CHAR(10) referencing DataFlow(RecordRegistry), linking this profile to a particular data flow.
  cross|DataProfile|RiskJoin: INT referencing RiskManagement(RiskTrace). Associates the data profile with a relevant risk record.
  cross|DataProfile|RecordRegistry: CHAR(10), an optional cross-reference, formerly 'RecordID'.
  cross|DataProfile|DataType: VARCHAR(80) describing the category of data (financial, personal, logs, etc.). Currently possible values: (Commercial, Personal, Financial, Industrial, Medical).
  cross|DataProfile|DataSense: VARCHAR(30) designating data sensitivity level (High, Medium, Low). Currently possible values: (High, Low, Critical, Medium).
  cross|DataProfile|VolGB: NUMERIC(10,2) representing the approximate data volume in gigabytes.
  cross|DataProfile|RecTally: BIGINT counting how many records exist in this data set or flow.
  cross|DataProfile|SubjTally: BIGINT enumerating how many unique data subjects are impacted/contained.
  cross|DataProfile|RetDays: INT storing the retention duration (in days) for this data.
  cross|DataProfile|FormatType: VARCHAR(80) naming the data format (CSV, JSON, XML, proprietary, etc.). Currently possible values: (Mixed, Unstructured, Structured).
  cross|DataProfile|QltyScore: NUMERIC(4,2) capturing data quality on a custom 0–100 scale.
  cross|DataProfile|IntCheck: checkstatus_enum enumerating data integrity check status. Currently possible values: (Passed, Failed, Partial).
  cross|DataProfile|CsumVerify: checkstatus_enum enumerating checksum verification status. Currently possible values: (Failed, Success, Pending).
  cross|DataProfile|SrcValState: checkstatus_enum enumerating source validation status. Currently possible values: (Pending, Verified, Failed).
  cross|DataProfile|DestValState: checkstatus_enum enumerating destination validation status. Currently possible values: (Pending, Verified, Failed).
  cross|SecurityProfile|SecurityTrace: SERIAL primary key uniquely identifying each security profile record.
  cross|SecurityProfile|FlowKey: CHAR(10) referencing DataFlow(RecordRegistry). Associates security details with a data flow.
  cross|SecurityProfile|RiskKey: INT referencing RiskManagement(RiskTrace). Links security measures to a risk record.
  cross|SecurityProfile|ProfileKey: INT referencing DataProfile(ProfileTrace). Ties security info to a data profile.
  cross|SecurityProfile|RecordRegistry: CHAR(10) optional cross-reference historically called 'RecordID'.
  cross|SecurityProfile|EncState: encryptionstatus_enum. Possible values: (Full, Partial).
  cross|SecurityProfile|EncMeth: VARCHAR(40) describing the encryption method used (AES, RSA, etc.). Currently possible values: (SM4, Custom, RSA-2048, AES-256).
  cross|SecurityProfile|KeyManState: VARCHAR(40) for key management status. Currently possible values: (Hybrid, Distributed, Centralized).
  cross|SecurityProfile|MaskLevel: partialnone_enum enumerating data masking. Currently possible values: (Partial, Full).
  cross|SecurityProfile|AnonMeth: VARCHAR(40) describing the anonymization methodology. Currently possible values: (T-Closeness, K-Anonymity, L-Diversity).
  cross|SecurityProfile|PsymState: VARCHAR(40) describing pseudonymization status. Currently possible values: (Partial, Applied).
  cross|SecurityProfile|AuthMeth: VARCHAR(40) specifying the authentication method (Basic, OAuth, SAML, etc.). Currently possible values: (Basic, SSO, MFA).
  cross|SecurityProfile|AuthzFrame: VARCHAR(45) specifying the authorization framework (RBAC, ABAC, etc.). Currently possible values: (ABAC, Custom, RBAC).
  cross|SecurityProfile|AclState: VARCHAR(30) describing the current access control status. Currently possible values: (Adequate, Strong, Weak).
  cross|SecurityProfile|ApiSecState: VARCHAR(30) enumerating the API security posture. Currently possible values: (Vulnerable, Secure, Review Required).
  cross|SecurityProfile|LogIntCheck: VARCHAR(30) specifying the log integrity check mechanism. Currently possible values: (Pending, Passed, Failed).
  cross|SecurityProfile|LogRetDays: SMALLINT indicating how many days audit logs are retained.
  cross|SecurityProfile|BkpState: VARCHAR(35) describing the backup status. Currently possible values: (Current, Failed, Outdated).
  cross|SecurityProfile|DRecState: VARCHAR(35) describing the disaster recovery status. Currently possible values: (Untested, Tested, Missing).
  cross|SecurityProfile|BCState: VARCHAR(35) storing the business continuity status or plan stage. Currently possible values: (Active, Outdated, Review Required).
  cross|VendorManagement|VendorTrace: SERIAL primary key uniquely identifying each vendor management record.
  cross|VendorManagement|SecJoin: INT referencing SecurityProfile(SecurityTrace). Ties vendor data with a security profile.
  cross|VendorManagement|RiskAssoc: INT referencing RiskManagement(RiskTrace). Connects vendor data to risk management info.
  cross|VendorManagement|RecordRegistry: CHAR(10) an optional cross-ref field, historically 'RecordID'.
  cross|VendorManagement|VendAssess: VARCHAR(40) describing vendor assessment status. Currently possible values: (Completed, In Progress, Due).
  cross|VendorManagement|VendSecRate: securityrating_enum enumerating vendor security rating. Currently possible values: (A, B, C, D).
  cross|VendorManagement|VendAudDate: DATE indicating when the vendor was last audited.
  cross|VendorManagement|ContrState: VARCHAR(30) capturing the contract state. Currently possible values: (Active, Under Review, Expired).
  cross|VendorManagement|ContrExpire: DATE specifying when the current contract expires.
  cross|VendorManagement|DpaState: VARCHAR(30) referencing Data Processing Agreement status. Currently possible values: (Required, Signed, Pending).
  cross|VendorManagement|SccState: VARCHAR(30) referencing Standard Contractual Clauses status. Currently possible values: (Implemented, Partial, Not Required).
  cross|VendorManagement|BcrState: VARCHAR(30) capturing the status of Binding Corporate Rules. Currently possible values: (Approved, Pending, Not Applicable).
  cross|VendorManagement|DocuState: VARCHAR(30) describing the vendor’s documentation completeness. Currently possible values: (Complete, Incomplete, Partial).
  cross|VendorManagement|PolComp: VARCHAR(30) enumerating vendor’s policy compliance. Currently possible values: (Partial, Full, Non-compliant).
  cross|VendorManagement|ProcComp: VARCHAR(30) enumerating vendor’s procedure compliance status. Currently possible values: (Non-compliant, Full, Partial).
  cross|VendorManagement|TrainState: VARCHAR(30) referencing the training status of vendor employees. Currently possible values: (Due, Overdue, Current).
  cross|VendorManagement|CertState: VARCHAR(30) referencing certifications the vendor holds. Currently possible values: (Pending, Expired, Valid).
  cross|VendorManagement|MonState: VARCHAR(30) storing the vendor’s monitoring status or approach. Currently possible values: (Inactive, Partial, Active).
  cross|VendorManagement|RepState: VARCHAR(30) capturing the vendor’s reporting requirements or status. Currently possible values: (Delayed, Current, Overdue).
  cross|VendorManagement|StakeComm: TEXT detailing stakeholder communication or engagement strategy for the vendor relationship. Currently possible values: (Limited, Poor, Regular).
  cross|Compliance|ComplianceTrace: SERIAL primary key uniquely identifying each compliance record.
  cross|Compliance|RiskTie: INT referencing RiskManagement(RiskTrace). Links compliance data to a specific risk record.
  cross|Compliance|VendorTie: INT referencing VendorManagement(VendorTrace). Connects compliance info to a specific vendor record.
  cross|Compliance|RecordRegistry: CHAR(10) optionally referencing an older 'RecordID'. May be used for cross references.
  cross|Compliance|LegalBase: VARCHAR(150) describing the legal basis for data processing (e.g., 'Consent', 'Legal Obligation'). Currently possible values: (Legal Obligation, Contract, Legitimate Interest, Consent).
  cross|Compliance|ConsentState: VARCHAR(30) capturing the status of consent (e.g., 'Obtained', 'Revoked'). Currently possible values: (Not Required, Valid, Expired, Pending).
  cross|Compliance|ConsentColl: DATE indicating when user consent was collected.
  cross|Compliance|ConsentExp: DATE indicating when that consent expires or needs renewal.
  cross|Compliance|PurpLimit: VARCHAR(300) specifying the purpose limitation or constraints (GDPR principle). Currently possible values: (General, Multiple, Specific).
  cross|Compliance|PurpDesc: TEXT describing the purpose for data processing in detail. Currently possible values: (Business Operations, Research, Marketing, Compliance).
  cross|Compliance|GdprComp: compliancelevel_enum enumerating GDPR compliance level. Currently possible values: (Partial, Non-compliant, Compliant).
  cross|Compliance|CcpaComp: compliancelevel_enum enumerating CCPA compliance level. Currently possible values: (Compliant, Non-compliant, Partial).
  cross|Compliance|PiplComp: compliancelevel_enum enumerating PIPL compliance level. Currently possible values: (Non-compliant, Partial, Compliant).
  cross|Compliance|LocLawComp: compliancelevel_enum enumerating local law compliance. Currently possible values: (Non-compliant, Compliant, Partial).
  cross|Compliance|RegApprovals: VARCHAR(300) listing any regulatory approvals or licenses obtained. Currently possible values: (Obtained, Not Required, Pending).
  cross|Compliance|PrivImpAssess: TEXT capturing the privacy impact assessment details or outcome. Currently possible values: (Completed, In Progress, Required).
  cross|Compliance|DataSubjRight: VARCHAR(40) describing data subject rights or status (access, erasure, portability, etc.). Currently possible values: (Partial, Fully Supported, Limited).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|AuditTrace: SERIAL primary key uniquely identifying each audit-and-compliance record.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|ProfJoin: INT referencing DataProfile(ProfileTrace). Ties this audit to a specific data profile.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|CompJoin: INT referencing Compliance(ComplianceTrace). Links the audit to a compliance record.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|VendJoin: INT referencing VendorManagement(VendorTrace). Connects this record to a specific vendor.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|RecordRegistry: CHAR(10) optional cross-reference for older 'RecordID'.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|AudTrailState: auditstatus_enum enumerating the audit trail status. Currently possible values: (Complete, Missing, Partial).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|FindTally: SMALLINT storing the count of audit findings identified.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|CritFindNum: SMALLINT counting how many critical findings were discovered.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|RemedState: VARCHAR(40) describing remediation status (e.g., 'Pending', 'In Progress'). Currently possible values: (In Progress, Not Started, Completed).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|RemedDue: DATE specifying the deadline for remediation actions.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|AuthNotify: VARCHAR(40) describing if/when authorities must be notified. Currently possible values: (Not Required, Required, Submitted).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|BorderMech: VARCHAR(40) capturing cross-border mechanism (SCC, BCR, etc.) if relevant. Currently possible values: (SCCs, Adequacy Decision, Derogations, BCRs).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|TransImpAssess: TEXT describing any transfer impact assessment for cross-border data flows. Currently possible values: (Required, Completed, In Progress).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|LocalReqs: TEXT detailing local requirements or constraints on the data flow. Currently possible values: (Not Met, Met, Partial).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|DataMapState: VARCHAR(40) describing the data mapping status (complete, partial, etc.). Currently possible values: (Partial, Complete, Outdated).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|SysIntState: VARCHAR(40) referencing the system integration status. Currently possible values: (Fully Integrated, Manual, Partial).
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|AccReqNum: SMALLINT counting how many access requests have been received.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|DelReqNum: SMALLINT counting how many data deletion requests were filed.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|RectReqNum: SMALLINT counting how many rectification (correction) requests were made.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|PortReqNum: SMALLINT counting how many data portability requests were received.
  cross|AuditAndCompliance|RespTimeDay: NUMERIC(4,1) capturing the average or allowed response time (days) to subject requests.

Knowledge definitions:
[0] Data Transfer Efficiency (DTE): DTE = \frac{\text{SuccessPct}}{\text{ErrTally} + 1}
[1] Bandwidth Saturation Index (BSI): BSI = \text{BwidthPct} \times \frac{\text{DataSizeMB}}{\text{DurMin}}
[2] Risk Exposure Score (RES): RES = \text{RiskAssess} \times \text{CtrlEff}^{-1}
[3] Compliance Cost Ratio (CCR): CCR = \frac{\text{CostUSD}}{\text{PenUSD} + 1}
[5] Security Robustness Score (SRS): SRS = \begin{cases} 3 & \text{if EncState = 'Full' and AclState = 'Strong'} \\ 2 & \text{if EncState = 'Full' or AclState = 'Adequate'} \\ 1 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}
[6] Vendor Reliability Index (VRI): VRI = \text{VendSecRate value} \times \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if ContrState = 'Active'} \\ 0.5 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}
[7] Audit Finding Severity (AFS): AFS = \frac{\text{CritFindNum}}{\text{FindTally} + 1}
[8] Data Subject Request Load (DSRL): DSRL = \text{AccReqNum} + \text{DelReqNum} + \text{RectReqNum} + \text{PortReqNum}
[9] Cross-Border Risk Factor (CBRF): CBRF = \text{RES} \times \begin{cases} 2 & \text{if OrigNation \neq DestNation} \\ 1 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}
[10] High-Risk Data Flow: A data flow where RES > 0.7 and DSI > 100
[11] Secure Data Flow: A data flow where SRS = 3
[12] Non-Compliant Vendor: A vendor where PolComp = 'Non-compliant' or ProcComp = 'Non-compliant'
[13] Critical Audit Issue: An audit where AFS > 0.5
[14] Sensitive Data Exposure: A data profile where DSI > 100 and SRS < 2
[15] Cross-Border Compliance Gap: A compliance record where GdprComp = 'Non-compliant' or LocLawComp = 'Non-compliant' and OrigNation ≠ DestNation
[16] Vendor Risk Tier: A vendor is: High Risk if VRI < 2, Medium Risk if 2 ≤ VRI < 3, Low Risk if VRI ≥ 3
[17] Data Integrity Failure: A data profile where IntCheck = 'Failed' or CsumVerify = 'Failed'
[18] Overloaded Data Flow: A data flow where BSI > 50 and DTE < 1.0
[19] Regulatory Risk Exposure: A data flow with CBRF > 1.5 and Cross-Border Compliance Gap exists
[20] DataFlow.SuccessPct: Ranges from 0 to 100%. A SuccessPct of 95% indicates reliable transfers, while <80% suggests frequent failures, from DataFlow.
[21] RiskManagement.RiskAssess: Ranges from 0 to 100. A RiskAssess > 80 indicates high risk, while <20 suggests minimal risk, from RiskManagement.
[22] DataProfile.VolGB: Ranges from 0 to millions. A VolGB of 1000 might represent a large dataset, while 0.1 is typical for small logs, from DataProfile.
[23] SecurityProfile.EncState: Enum: 'Full', 'Partial'. 'Full' indicates all data is encrypted, while 'Partial' suggests gaps, from SecurityProfile.
[24] VendorManagement.VendSecRate: Enum: 'A' = 4, 'B' = 3, 'C' = 2, 'D' or others = 1. This numeric scale quantifies vendor security, where 'A' reflects top-tier security (score 4), and lower ratings (down to 'D') indicate progressively weaker security controls.
[25] Compliance.GdprComp: Enum: 'Compliant', 'Non-compliant', 'Partial'. 'Compliant' meets all GDPR rules, 'Non-compliant' fails key requirements, from Compliance.
[26] AuditAndCompliance.RespTimeDay: Ranges from 0 to days. A RespTimeDay of 1.5 suggests quick responses, while >7 indicates delays, from AuditAndCompliance.
[27] DataFlow.ErrTally: Integer ≥ 0. An ErrTally of 0 indicates flawless transfers, while >10 suggests reliability issues, from DataFlow.
[28] RiskManagement.CtrlEff: Ranges from 0 to 100. A CtrlEff of 90% shows strong controls, while <50% indicates weaknesses, from RiskManagement.
[29] SecurityProfile.LogRetDays: Integer ≥ 0 days. A LogRetDays of 365 meets many compliance needs, while <30 may violate regulations, from SecurityProfile.
[30] Data Flow Reliability Score (DFRS): DFRS = \text{DTE} \times (1 - \text{RtryTally} / (\text{ErrTally} + 1))
[31] Security Control Cost Ratio (SCCR): SCCR = \text{SRS} / (\text{CostUSD} + 1)
[32] Vendor Compliance Burden (VCB): VCB = \text{AFS} \times (5 - \text{VendSecRate value})
[33] Cross-Border Data Volume Risk (CDVR): CDVR = \text{CBRF} \times \text{VolGB}
[34] Data Subject Request Pressure (DSRP): DSRP = \text{DSRL} \times \text{RespTimeDay}
[35] Encryption Coverage Ratio (ECR): ECR = \text{SRS} \times \text{DSI}
[36] Audit Remediation Load (ARL): ARL = \text{AFS} \times \text{DSRL}
[37] Bandwidth Risk Factor (BRF): BRF = \text{BSI} \times \text{DSI}
[38] Vendor Risk Amplification (VRA): VRA = \text{VRI} \times \text{RES}
[39] Critical Data Flow Risk: A data flow where RES > 0.7 and DFRS < 0.5
[40] Overburdened Compliance Flow: A data flow where CCR > 0.8 and ARL > 10
[41] Unprotected Sensitive Data: A data profile where DSI > 100 and ECR < 2
[42] High-Pressure Data Flow: A data flow where DSRP > 50 and BSI > 50
[43] Vendor-Driven Risk Flow: A data flow where VRA > 3 and VCB > 2
[44] Cross-Border Audit Risk: A data flow where CDVR > 1000 and AFS > 0.5
[45] Insecure High-Volume Flow: A data flow where VolGB > 500 and SRS < 2
[46] Regulatory Overload Flow: A data flow with Regulatory Risk Exposure and GdprComp = 'Non-compliant'
[47] Bandwidth-Constrained Risk: A data flow where BRF > 100 and RES > 0.7
[48] Incident Resolution Efficiency (IRE): IRE = \text{SLApct} / (\text{AvgResolHrs} + 1), \text{where SLApct and AvgResolHrs are from RiskManagement, adding 1 to avoid division by zero.}
[49] Incident-Prone Data Flow: A data flow where IRE < 0.5 and High-Risk Data Flow
[50] Data Flow Stability Index (DFSI): DFSI = \text{DFRS} \times \frac{\text{SuccessPct}}{\text{ErrTally} + 1}
[51] Compliance Overhead Ratio (COR): COR = \text{DSRP} / (\text{CostUSD} + 1)
[52] Security Posture Maturity (SPM): SPM = \text{ECR} \times \frac{\text{LogRetDays}}{365}
[53] Vendor Risk Concentration (VRC): VRC = \text{VRA} \times (1 - \text{VRI})
[54] Cross-Border Compliance Exposure (CBCE): CBCE = \text{CDVR} \times \begin{cases} 2 & \text{if GdprComp = 'Non-compliant'} \\ 1 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}
[55] Incident Impact Factor (IIF): IIF = \text{RES} \times (1 - \text{IRE})
[56] Data Retention Risk Score (DRRS): DRRS = \text{DSI} \times \frac{\text{RetDays}}{365}
[57] Audit Compliance Pressure (ACP): ACP = \text{ARL} \times \text{AFS}
[58] Bandwidth Compliance Risk (BCR): BCR = \text{BRF} \times \begin{cases} 1.5 & \text{if GdprComp = 'Partial'} \\ 2 & \text{if GdprComp = 'Non-compliant'} \\ 1 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}
[59] Vendor Security Cost Index (VSCI): VSCI = \text{VCB} / (\text{SCCR} + 1)
[60] Unstable High-Risk Flow: A data flow where DFSI < 0.5 and Critical Data Flow Risk exists
[61] Overloaded Security Flow: A data flow where SPM < 1 and CBCE > 100
[62] Excessive Retention Risk: A data profile where DRRS > 50 and DSI > 100
[63] Vendor Compliance Risk Cluster: A vendor where VRC > 2 and VCB > 2
[64] Incident-Prone Compliance Flow: A data flow where IIF > 0.8 and GdprComp = 'Non-compliant'
[65] Audit-Stressed Data Flow: A data flow where ACP > 5 and COR > 0.5.
[66] Bandwidth-Limited Compliance Risk: A data flow where BCR > 50 and Cross-Border Compliance Gap exists
[67] Costly Vendor Risk Flow: A data flow where VSCI > 1 and VRA > 3
[68] Sensitive Unstable Flow: A data flow where DFSI < 0.5 and Sensitive Data Exposure exists
[69] High-Impact Audit Risk Flow: A data flow where Regulatory Risk Exposure exists and ACP > 5
[70] Transfer Path: A string concatenating OrigNation and DestNation as 'OrigNation -> DestNation'
[71] Request Breakdown: An array of strings listing request types and their counts: 'Access: AccReqNum', 'Deletion: DelReqNum', 'Rectification: RectReqNum', 'Portability: PortReqNum', unnested for display
[72] Integrity Failure Count (IFC): IFC = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if IntCheck = 'Failed'} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} + \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if CsumVerify = 'Failed'} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}
[73] Failure Types List: A comma-separated string listing failure types: 'Integrity Check' if IntCheck = 'Failed', 'Checksum Verification' if CsumVerify = 'Failed'.
[74] High Audit Compliance Pressure: A data flow where ACP > 5
[75] Cross-Border Data Flow: A data flow where OrigNation != DestNation
[76] Slow Remediation Timeline: A data flow where CURRENT_DATE - RemedDue > 0
[77] Nearing Remediation Deadline: A data flow where (CURRENT_DATE - RemedDue) is between -5 and 0
[78] High Vendor Risk Concentration: A data flow where CURRENT_DATE - RemedDue > 0

User query:
List the top 5 high-risk data flows, showing each flows ID, risk score, and data sensitivity. Sort by data sensitivity.

Your earlier analysis:
**Relevant Tables and Columns:**
1. **dataflow**
   - `recordregistry` (flow ID)
2. **riskmanagement**
   - `riskassess` (risk score)
3. **dataprofile**
   - `datasense` (data sensitivity)

**Terms Needing Clarification:**
- "high-risk data flows": Define the criteria for "high-risk" (e.g., specific thresholds for risk score).
- "data sensitivity": Clarify the specific values or categories of sensitivity that should be considered (e.g., High, Medium, Low).

Clarification:
  Q: What specific threshold or criteria do you consider to define "high-risk" for the risk score?
  A: For defining "high-risk" in terms of the risk score, I consider a risk score greater than 2. This threshold is based on the calculation of the risk score using the formula: `COALESCE(rm.RiskAssess / NULLIF(rm.CtrlEff, 0), 0)`. Any data flow that results in a risk score above this value should be classified as high-risk.

Write the SQL query:

output:
SELECT df.recordregistry AS flow_id, 
       COALESCE(rm.riskassess / NULLIF(rm.ctrl_eff, 0), 0) AS risk_score, 
       dp.datasense AS data_sensitivity
FROM dataflow df
JOIN riskmanagement rm ON df.recordregistry = rm.flowlink
JOIN dataprofile dp ON df.recordregistry = dp.flowsign
WHERE COALESCE(rm.riskassess / NULLIF(rm.ctrl_eff, 0), 0) > 2
ORDER BY dp.datasense
LIMIT 5;

Column descriptions:
  alien|Observatories|ObservStation: Full name: 'Observatory Name'. Explanation: This field holds the name or unique identifier for the observatory station. Data type: CHAR(60). Example: 'OBS_STATION_ALPHA'.
  alien|Observatories|WeathProfile: Full name: 'Weather Profile'. Explanation: A short description of weather conditions during observation. Data type: VARCHAR(40). Possible categories: Clear, Cloudy, Partially Cloudy.
  alien|Observatories|SeeingProfile: Full name: 'Seeing Profile'. Explanation: Assessment of sky seeing conditions (atmospheric steadiness). Data type: VARCHAR(50). Possible categories: Excellent, Good, Poor.
  alien|Observatories|AtmosTransparency: Full name: 'Atmospheric Transparency'. Explanation: A numeric measure of how transparent the atmosphere is. Data type: NUMERIC(5,3). Example: '0.867'.
  alien|Observatories|LunarStage: Full name: 'Lunar Phase'. Explanation: The current phase of the Moon during observation. Data type: VARCHAR(25). Possible categories: First Quarter, Full, Last Quarter, New.
  alien|Observatories|LunarDistDeg: Full name: 'Moon Distance (Degrees)'. Explanation: Angular distance to the Moon in degrees. Data type: DECIMAL(7,2). Example: '97.52'.
  alien|Observatories|SolarStatus: Full name: 'Solar Activity'. Explanation: The level of solar activity at the time of observation. Data type: VARCHAR(35). Possible categories: High, Low, Moderate.
  alien|Observatories|GeomagStatus: Full name: 'Geomagnetic Activity'. Explanation: The level of geomagnetic activity during observation. Data type: VARCHAR(35). Possible categories: Active, Quiet, Storm.
  alien|Observatories|SiderealLocal: Full name: 'Local Sidereal Time'. Explanation: Sidereal time at the observatory in HH:MM:SS format. Data type: CHAR(8). Example: '12:34:56'.
  alien|Observatories|AirTempC: Full name: 'Air Temperature (°C)'. Explanation: Ambient temperature in Celsius. Data type: NUMERIC(5,2). Example: '18.45'.
  alien|Observatories|HumidityRate: Full name: 'Humidity (%)'. Explanation: Relative humidity as a percentage. Data type: NUMERIC(6,3). Example: '62.300'.
  alien|Observatories|WindSpeedMs: Full name: 'Wind Speed (m/s)'. Explanation: Wind speed in meters per second. Data type: NUMERIC(4,2). Example: '3.45'.
  alien|Observatories|PressHpa: Full name: 'Pressure (hPa)'. Explanation: Atmospheric pressure in hectopascals. Data type: DECIMAL(6,1). Example: '1013.2'.
  alien|Telescopes|TelescRegistry: Full name: 'Telescope Registry'. Explanation: A unique identifier for the telescope. Data type: CHAR(20). Example: 'TELESC_0001'.
  alien|Telescopes|ObservStation: Full name: 'Observatory Name Reference'. Explanation: Foreign key linking to the observatory station. Data type: CHAR(60). Example: 'OBS_STATION_ALPHA'.
  alien|Telescopes|EquipStatus: Full name: 'Equipment Status'. Explanation: Operational state of the telescope. Data type: VARCHAR(35). Possible categories: Degraded, Maintenance, Operational.
  alien|Telescopes|CalibrStatus: Full name: 'Calibration Status'. Explanation: Status of telescope’s calibration. Data type: VARCHAR(50). Possible categories: Current, Due, Overdue.
  alien|Telescopes|PointAccArc: Full name: 'Pointing Accuracy (arcsec)'. Explanation: How precise the telescope can point, in arcseconds. Data type: NUMERIC(6,2). Example: '0.45'.
  alien|Telescopes|TrackAccArc: Full name: 'Tracking Accuracy (arcsec)'. Explanation: How accurately the telescope can track a target, in arcseconds. Data type: NUMERIC(6,2). Example: '1.20'.
  alien|Telescopes|FocusQuality: Full name: 'Focus Quality'. Explanation: Quality of the telescope’s focusing system. Data type: VARCHAR(25). Possible categories: Excellent, Good, Poor.
  alien|Telescopes|DetectTempK: Full name: 'Detector Temperature (K)'. Explanation: Temperature of the telescope’s primary detector in Kelvin. Data type: DECIMAL(7,2). Example: '123.45'.
  alien|Telescopes|CoolSysStatus: Full name: 'Cooling System Status'. Explanation: Status of the telescope’s cooling system. Data type: VARCHAR(35). Possible categories: Critical, Normal, Warning.
  alien|Telescopes|PowerStatus: Full name: 'Power Supply Status'. Explanation: Indicates which power source or level is in use. Data type: VARCHAR(30). Possible categories: Backup, Critical, Main.
  alien|Telescopes|DataStorStatus: Full name: 'Data Storage Status'. Explanation: Capacity and status of local data storage. Data type: VARCHAR(35). Possible categories: Available, Critical, Low.
  alien|Telescopes|NetStatus: Full name: 'Network Status'. Explanation: Status of the network connection for telescope data transfer. Data type: VARCHAR(40). Possible categories: Connected, Disconnected, Limited.
  alien|Telescopes|BandUsagePct: Full name: 'Bandwidth Usage (%)'. Explanation: Network bandwidth usage as a percentage. Data type: NUMERIC(5,2). Example: '73.25'.
  alien|Telescopes|ProcQueueStatus: Full name: 'Processing Queue Status'. Explanation: Indicates how full the processing queue is. Data type: VARCHAR(40). Possible categories: Empty, Full, Normal.
  alien|Signals|SignalRegistry: Full name: 'Signal Registry'. Explanation: Unique ID for each signal record. Data type: CHAR(36). Example: 'SIG-123E4567-E89B'.
  alien|Signals|TimeMark: Full name: 'Timestamp'. Explanation: The moment of signal detection. In some CSVs, it may be stored as an Excel serial date/time (e.g., '44302.55648' ≈ 2021-05-16 13:20:20 UTC). Data type: TIMESTAMPTZ. Example: '2025-08-01 13:45:00+00'.
  alien|Signals|TelescRef: Full name: 'Telescope Reference'. Explanation: Foreign key to the telescope used for detection. Data type: CHAR(20). Example: 'TELESC_0001'.
  alien|Signals|DetectInstr: Full name: 'Detection Instrument'. Explanation: The instrument type employed for detecting the signal. Data type: VARCHAR(50). Possible categories: Infrared Array, Optical Telescope, Quantum Detector, Radio Telescope.
  alien|Signals|SignalClass: Full name: 'Signal Type'. Explanation: Broad classification of the detected signal. Data type: VARCHAR(50). Possible categories: Broadband, Continuous, Modulated, Narrowband, Pulsed.
  alien|Signals|SigStrDb: Full name: 'Signal Strength (dB)'. Explanation: Measured strength of the signal in decibels. Data type: NUMERIC(7,2). Example: '12.35'.
  alien|Signals|FreqMhz: Full name: 'Frequency (MHz)'. Explanation: Signal’s nominal frequency in MHz. Data type: DECIMAL(9,3). Example: '1420.405'.
  alien|Signals|BwHz: Full name: 'Bandwidth (Hz)'. Explanation: Signal’s bandwidth in Hertz. Data type: DECIMAL(10,3). Example: '500000.000'.
  alien|Signals|CenterFreqMhz: Full name: 'Center Frequency (MHz)'. Explanation: Center frequency of the detected signal in MHz. Data type: NUMERIC(8,3). Example: '1420.500'.
  alien|Signals|FreqDriftHzs: Full name: 'Frequency Drift (Hz/s)'. Explanation: How fast the signal drifts in frequency over time. Data type: NUMERIC(9,3). Example: '0.123'.
  alien|Signals|DoppShiftHz: Full name: 'Doppler Shift (Hz)'. Explanation: Measured Doppler shift of the signal in Hertz. Data type: DOUBLE PRECISION. Example: '142.75'.
  alien|Signals|SigDurSec: Full name: 'Signal Duration (s)'. Explanation: Total duration of the signal in seconds. Data type: NUMERIC(6,2). Example: '12.50'.
  alien|Signals|PulsePerSec: Full name: 'Pulse Rate (pulses/sec)'. Explanation: Rate of pulse repetition in the signal. Data type: NUMERIC(6,3). Example: '4.500'.
  alien|Signals|PulseWidMs: Full name: 'Pulse Width (ms)'. Explanation: Duration of each pulse in milliseconds. Data type: NUMERIC(6,3). Example: '2.300'.
  alien|Signals|ModType: Full name: 'Modulation Type'. Explanation: The method of signal modulation. Data type: VARCHAR(30). Possible categories: AM, FM, PM, QAM, Unknown.
  alien|Signals|ModIndex: Full name: 'Modulation Index'. Explanation: Numerical index describing modulation depth. Data type: DECIMAL(6,4). Example: '0.2875'.
  alien|Signals|CarrierFreqMhz: Full name: 'Carrier Frequency (MHz)'. Explanation: Frequency of the carrier wave in MHz. Data type: DECIMAL(9,3). Example: '1000.000'.
  alien|Signals|PhaseShiftDeg: Full name: 'Phase Shift (°)'. Explanation: Phase shift in degrees for the signal. Data type: NUMERIC(6,2). Example: '45.00'.
  alien|Signals|PolarMode: Full name: 'Polarization Mode'. Explanation: Type of signal polarization. Data type: VARCHAR(30). Possible categories: Circular, Elliptical, Linear, Unknown.
  alien|Signals|PolarAngleDeg: Full name: 'Polarization Angle (°)'. Explanation: Angle of polarization in degrees. Data type: DECIMAL(5,1). Example: '90.0'.
  alien|Signals|SnrRatio: Full name: 'Signal-to-Noise Ratio'. Explanation: SNR measured for the signal. Data type: DECIMAL(6,2). Example: '18.75'.
  alien|Signals|NoiseFloorDbm: Full name: 'Noise Floor (dBm)'. Explanation: Measured noise floor in dBm. Data type: DOUBLE PRECISION. Example: '-85.3'.
  alien|Signals|InterfLvl: Full name: 'Interference Level'. Explanation: Degree of interference around the signal. Data type: VARCHAR(30). Possible categories: High, Low, Medium, None.
  alien|Signals|RfiStat: Full name: 'RFI Status'. Explanation: Radio Frequency Interference status. Data type: VARCHAR(30). Possible categories: Clean, Contaminated, Unknown.
  alien|Signals|AtmoInterf: Full name: 'Atmospheric Interference'. Explanation: Impact of atmospheric conditions on signal. Data type: VARCHAR(30). Possible categories: Minimal, Moderate, Severe.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|SignalRef: Full name: 'Signal Registry Reference'. Explanation: Primary key referencing the main signal record. Data type: CHAR(36). Example: 'SIG-XYZ-9876'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|FalsePosProb: Full name: 'False Positive Probability'. Explanation: Probability that the signal is falsely detected. Data type: DECIMAL(5,4). Example: '0.0135'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|SigUnique: Full name: 'Signal Uniqueness'. Explanation: A measure of how unique the signal is compared to others. Data type: DECIMAL(7,4). Example: '99.1234'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|SimIndex: Full name: 'Similarity Index'. Explanation: Compares the signal to known references. Data type: NUMERIC(5,4). Example: '0.8222'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|CorrScore: Full name: 'Correlation Score'. Explanation: How well the signal aligns with expected patterns. Data type: DECIMAL(5,4). Example: '0.9950'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|AnomScore: Full name: 'Anomaly Score'. Explanation: How unusual or unexpected the signal is. Data type: FLOAT. Example: '1.23'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|TechSigProb: Full name: 'Technosignature Probability'. Explanation: Probability that the signal is of artificial technological origin. Data type: DECIMAL(5,4). Example: '0.6789'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|BioSigProb: Full name: 'Biosignature Probability'. Explanation: Probability that the signal indicates a biological origin. Data type: DECIMAL(6,2). Example: '45.21'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|NatSrcProb: Full name: 'Natural Source Probability'. Explanation: Probability the signal is from a natural source. Data type: NUMERIC(7,3). Example: '98.761'.
  alien|SignalProbabilities|ArtSrcProb: Full name: 'Artificial Source Probability'. Explanation: Probability the signal is from an artificial source. Data type: NUMERIC(3,1). Example: '3.4'.
  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|SignalRef: Full name: 'Signal Registry Reference'. Explanation: Primary key referencing the main signal. Data type: CHAR(36). Example: 'SIG-ABC-1234'.

  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|GravLens: Full name: 'Gravitational Lensing'. Explanation: Whether the signal is affected by gravitational lensing. Data type: VARCHAR(50). Possible categories: None, Strong, Weak.
  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|QuantEffects: Full name: 'Quantum Effects'. Explanation: Quantum-level phenomena observed in the signal. Data type: VARCHAR(85). Possible categories: None, Observed, Significant.
  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|EncryptEvid: Full name: 'Encryption Evidence'. Explanation: Whether there is indication the signal is encrypted. Data type: VARCHAR(40). Possible categories: None, Possible, Strong.
  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|LangStruct: Full name: 'Language Structure'. Explanation: Presence of linguistic or structured patterns. Data type: TEXT. Possible categories: Complex, None, Simple.
  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|MsgContent: Full name: 'Message Content'. Explanation: Indicates whether an actual message was detected. Data type: TEXT. Possible categories: Identified, None, Possible.
  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|CultSig: Full name: 'Cultural Significance'. Explanation: The level of cultural impact or interest. Data type: VARCHAR(60). Possible categories: High, Low, None.
  alien|SignalAdvancedPhenomena|SciImpact: Full name: 'Scientific Impact'. Explanation: Significance of the signal to scientific research. Data type: VARCHAR(50). Possible categories: Major, Minor, Moderate.
  alien|SignalClassification|SignalRef: Full name: 'Signal Registry Reference'. Explanation: Foreign key referencing the main signal. Data type: CHAR(36). Example: 'SIG-DEF-5678'.

  alien|SignalClassification|ClassConf: Full name: 'Classification Confidence (%)'. Explanation: How confident we are in the assigned signal class. Data type: DECIMAL(5,2). Example: '92.50'.
  alien|SignalDecoding|SignalRef: Full name: 'Signal Registry Reference'. Explanation: Foreign key referencing the main signal entry. Data type: CHAR(36). Example: 'SIG-GHI-9012'.

  alien|SignalDynamics|SignalRef: Full name: 'Signal Registry Reference'. Explanation: Foreign key referencing the main signal. Data type: CHAR(36). Example: 'SIG-JKL-3456'.

  alien|SignalDynamics|SigRecurr: Full name: 'Signal Recurrence'. Explanation: Whether the signal recurs over time. Data type: VARCHAR(25). Possible categories: None, Regular, Sporadic.
  alien|SignalDynamics|SigEvolve: Full name: 'Signal Evolution'. Explanation: Indicates if the signal changes/evolves during observation. Data type: VARCHAR(25). Possible categories: Dynamic, Static, Unknown.
  alien|SignalDynamics|TempStab: Full name: 'Temporal Stability'. Explanation: How stable the signal remains over time. Data type: VARCHAR(20). If no fixed categories, example: 'Stable'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|SpatStab: Full name: 'Spatial Stability'. Explanation: How stable the signal is spatially (e.g., consistent direction). Data type: VARCHAR(20). If no fixed categories, example: 'Moderate'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|FreqStab: Full name: 'Frequency Stability'. Explanation: How stable the signal’s frequency is. Data type: VARCHAR(35). If no fixed categories, example: 'HighlyStable'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|PhaseStab: Full name: 'Phase Stability'. Explanation: Any variation in signal phase. Data type: VARCHAR(35). If no fixed categories, example: 'VaryingPhase'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|AmpStab: Full name: 'Amplitude Stability'. Explanation: Consistency of signal amplitude. Data type: VARCHAR(20). If no fixed categories, example: 'Unstable'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|ModStab: Full name: 'Modulation Stability'. Explanation: Consistency in the signal’s modulation scheme. Data type: VARCHAR(30). If no fixed categories, example: 'Consistent'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|SigCoherence: Full name: 'Signal Coherence'. Explanation: How coherent the signal remains over its duration. Data type: VARCHAR(25). If no fixed categories, example: 'HighCoherence'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|SigDisp: Full name: 'Signal Dispersion'. Explanation: The degree to which the signal is dispersed (time/frequency smearing). Data type: VARCHAR(25). If no fixed categories, example: 'SignificantDisp'.
  alien|SignalDynamics|SigScint: Full name: 'Signal Scintillation'. Explanation: Fluctuation in signal amplitude due to propagation effects. Data type: VARCHAR(45). If no fixed categories, example: 'MildScintillation'.

  alien|ObservationalConditions|SignalRef: Full name: 'Signal Registry Reference'. Explanation: Primary key referencing the signal. Data type: CHAR(36). Example: 'SIG-PQR-9876'.

Knowledge definitions:
[0] Signal-to-Noise Quality Indicator (SNQI): $\text{SNQI} = \text{SnrRatio} - 0.1 \times |\text{NoiseFloorDbm}|$, where higher values indicate better detection quality. Positive values generally indicate analyzable signals.
[1] Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI): $\text{AOI} = \text{AtmosTransparency} \times (1 - \frac{\text{HumidityRate}}{100}) \times (1 - 0.02 \times \text{WindSpeedMs})$, where values closer to 1 indicate ideal observation conditions.
[2] Signal Complexity Ratio (SCR): $\text{SCR} = \frac{\text{ComplexIdx} \times \text{InfoDense}}{\log(\text{BwHz})}$, where higher values suggest potential artificial origin rather than natural phenomena.
[3] Technological Origin Likelihood Score (TOLS): $\text{TOLS} = \text{TechSigProb} \times (1 - \text{NatSrcProb}) \times \text{SigUnique} \times (0.5 + \frac{\text{AnomScore}}{10})$, where values above 0.75 warrant further investigation as potential technosignatures.
[4] Bandwidth-Frequency Ratio (BFR): $\text{BFR} = \frac{\text{BwHz}}{\text{CenterFreqMhz} \times 10^6}$, where narrow ratios ($<0.001$) often indicate technological signals while wider ratios suggest natural phenomena.
[5] Detection Instrument Sensitivity Factor (DISF): $\text{DISF} = (10 - \frac{|\text{AirTempC} - 15|}{10}) \times \text{AtmosTransparency} \times (1 - \frac{\text{HumidityRate}}{200}) \times \frac{100 - \text{LunarDistDeg}}{100}$, where values closer to 10 indicate optimal detection sensitivity.
[6] Encoding Complexity Index (ECI): $\text{ECI} = \frac{\text{CompressRatio} \times \text{ComplexIdx} \times \text{EntropyVal}}{10}$, where values above 1.5 suggest deliberate information encoding rather than random patterns.
[7] Signal Stability Metric (SSM): $\text{SSM} = (1 - \frac{|\text{FreqDriftHzs}|}{\text{FreqMhz} \times 1000}) \times \frac{\text{SigDurSec}}{1 + \frac{\text{DoppShiftHz}}{1000}}$, where higher values indicate more stable signals typical of fixed transmitters.
[8] Research Priority Index (RPI): $\text{RPI} = (\text{TechSigProb} \times 4 + \frac{\text{BioSigProb}}{100} + \text{SigUnique} \times 2 + \frac{\text{AnomScore}}{2}) \times (1 - \text{FalsePosProb})$, where values above 3 indicate high research priority.
[9] Lunar Interference Factor (LIF): $\text{LIF} = (1 - \frac{\text{LunarDistDeg}}{180}) \times (1 - \text{AtmosTransparency})$, where higher values indicate more lunar interference. Values above 0.5 suggest significant lunar contamination in data.
[10] Technosignature: A signal with $\text{TechSigProb} > 0.7$, $\text{NatSrcProb} < 0.3$, and $\text{ArtSrcProb} < 50$ that exhibits narrow bandwidth ($\text{BFR} < 0.001$) and high information density ($\text{InfoDense} > 0.8$).
[11] Coherent Information Pattern (CIP): Signals characterized by high signal stability ($\text{SSM} > 0.8$), organized information structure ($\text{EntropyVal}$ between 0.4-0.8), and consistent modulation ($\text{ModType}$ with $\text{ModIndex} > 0.5$).
[12] Target of Opportunity (TOO): Any signal with $\text{RPI} > 3.5$, $\text{TechSigProb} > 0.8$, and $\text{AnomScore} > 5$ that has not been previously documented or explained by known phenomena.
[13] Optimal Observing Window (OOW): Time periods when $\text{AOI} > 0.85$, $\text{LunarStage}$ is 'New' or 'First Quarter', $\text{LunarDistDeg} > 45$, and $\text{SolarStatus}$ is 'Low' or 'Moderate'.
[14] Signal Degradation Scenario (SDS): Observation conditions where one or more of: $\text{AtmosTransparency} < 0.7$, $\text{HumidityRate} > 70$, $\text{WindSpeedMs} > 8$, or $\text{GeomagStatus}$ contains 'Storm', resulting in compromised data quality.
[15] Narrowband Technological Marker (NTM): Signals with extremely narrow bandwidth ($\text{BFR} < 0.0001$), stable frequency ($\text{FreqDriftHzs} < 0.1$).
[16] Observational Confidence Level (OCL): A classification system with three tiers: 'High' ($\text{AOI} > 0.8$, $\text{EquipStatus} = \text{'Operational'}$, $\text{CalibrStatus} = \text{'Current'}$), 'Medium' ($\text{AOI}$ 0.5-0.8, minor equipment issues), and 'Low' ($\text{AOI} < 0.5$ or significant equipment problems).
[17] Potential Biosignature: Signals with $\text{BioSigProb} > 0.6$, $\text{TechSigProb} < 0.4$, and spectral features that match known biological emission patterns, often associated with specific molecular transitions.
[18] Encoded Information Transfer (EIT): Signals with $\text{ECI} > 1.8$, $\text{EntropyVal}$ between 0.3-0.7 (not random but structured), and consistent internal patterns that suggest language or data encoding schemes.
[19] Fast Radio Transient (FRT): Signals with extremely short duration ($\text{SigDurSec} < 0.1$), high signal strength ($\text{SigStrDb} > 15$), broad bandwidth ($\text{BwHz} > 1000000$), and no periodicity ($\text{RepeatCount} = 1$).
[20] WeathProfile: Clear: Indicates pristine sky conditions with no clouds, usually associated with $\text{AtmosTransparency} > 0.9$, low $\text{HumidityRate} (< 40\%)$, and minimal $\text{WindSpeedMs} (< 3.0)$. Provides ideal visibility for optical observations and minimal atmospheric interference for radio observations.
[21] SeeingProfile: Excellent: Describes atmospheric conditions with minimal turbulence, allowing for sharp, detailed observations. Typically corresponds to image stability better than 1 arcsecond and is often associated with stable temperature gradients and low wind speeds ($\text{WindSpeedMs} < 2.5$).
[22] SignalClass: Narrowband: Describes signals occupying a very narrow portion of the spectrum (typically $\text{BFR} < 0.0001$). Often associated with technological origins as natural sources rarely produce such spectrally confined emissions. These signals are particularly interesting in SETI research.
[23] GeomagStatus: Major Storm: Indicates intense solar-induced geomagnetic activity with Kp index ≥ 7. During such conditions, ionospheric perturbations significantly affect radio observations below 100 MHz, aurora may be visible at mid-latitudes, and satellite communications may experience disruptions.
[24] CIP Classification Label: Classification labels: 'Coherent Information Pattern Detected' ($\text{SSM} > 0.8$, $\text{EntropyVal}$ between 0.4-0.8, and $\text{ModIndex} > 0.5$), 'Potential Information Pattern' ($\text{SSM} > 0.6$ and $\text{EntropyVal}$ between 0.3-0.9$), or 'No Clear Pattern' (all other signals).
[25] SigClassType: Broadband Transient: Describes short-duration signals ($\text{SigDurSec}$ typically $< 5$) that span a large portion of the spectrum ($\text{BFR} > 0.1$). Examples include solar radio bursts, lightning discharges, and certain types of cosmic explosions like Fast Radio Bursts (FRBs).
[26] PolarMode: Circular: Describes electromagnetic waves where the electric field vector rotates in a circular pattern as the wave propagates. Circular polarization maintaining high purity across frequency (indicated by $\text{PolarAngleDeg}$ stability) is rare in natural sources and may indicate technological origin.
[27] EncryptEvid: Strong Pattern: Indicates detection of non-random, internally consistent patterns that resist simple decoding but show hallmarks of designed encryption or encoding. Characterized by high $\text{EntropyVal} (> 0.7)$ combined with structural regularity that defies natural explanation.
[28] EncodeType: Frequency Hopping: Describes a transmission technique where the signal rapidly switches frequencies according to a predetermined sequence. Detection would be characterized by discontinuous spectral features that follow a pattern. This technique is used on Earth to secure communications and reduce interference.
[29] FalsePosProb: <0.01: Indicates less than 1% probability that the signal is a false detection or artifact. Such low false positive probability typically results from multiple independent confirmations, excellent signal strength (high $\text{SnrRatio}$), and elimination of all known terrestrial and instrumental sources.
[30] Modulation Complexity Score (MCS): $\text{MCS} = \text{ModIndex} \times (1 + \text{SSM}) \times M_{\text{factor}}$, where $M_{\text{factor}}$ is 2 for $\text{ModType} = \text{'AM'}$, 1.5 for 'FM', and 1 for other types. Incorporates Signal Stability Metric (SSM) to weight stable modulations higher.
[31] Artificial Intelligence Detection Probability (AIDP): $\text{AIDP} = \frac{\text{ECI} \times \text{TOLS}}{1 + \text{NatSrcProb}}$, where ECI (Encoding Complexity Index) and TOLS (Technological Origin Likelihood Score) are weighted against natural source probability.
[32] Observation Quality Factor (OQF): $\text{OQF} = \text{AOI} \times (1 - \text{LIF}) \times (\text{PointAccArc} < 2 ? 1 : \frac{2}{\text{PointAccArc}})$, where AOI (Atmospheric Observability Index) and LIF (Lunar Interference Factor) are combined with telescope pointing accuracy.
[33] Information Entropy Ratio (IER): $\text{IER} = \frac{\text{EntropyVal}}{\text{NatSrcProb} \times 0.9 + 0.1}$, where values significantly greater than 1 suggest non-natural information content. Uses NatSrcProb as a baseline for expected natural entropy.
[34] Signal Processing Efficiency Index (SPEI): $\text{SPEI} = \frac{\text{DecodeIters} \times \text{ProcTimeHrs}}{\text{ECI} \times \text{ComplexIdx}}$, where ECI (Encoding Complexity Index) provides the complexity component to normalize processing time and iterations.
[35] Celestial Location Significance Factor (CLSF): $\text{CLSF} = (\text{CelestObj} ? 2 : 1) \times (\text{ObjType} == \text{'Giant'} \&\& \text{ObjMassSol} \text{ between } 0.8 \text{ and } 1.2 ? 1.5 : 1) \times (\text{ObjMetal} > 0 ? \text{ObjMetal} + 1 : 0.5)$, where higher values indicate source locations more likely to harbor intelligent life.
[36] Confirmation Confidence Score (CCS): $\text{CCS} = (1 - \text{FalsePosProb}) \times \text{DecodeConf} \times \text{ClassConf} \times (\text{SNQI} > 0 ? \frac{\text{SNQI}}{10} + 0.5 : 0.1)$, where SNQI (Signal-to-Noise Quality Indicator) provides a quality weighting factor.
[37] Habitable Zone Signal Relevance (HZSR): $\text{HZSR} = \text{TOLS} \times (\text{ObjType} == \text{'Dwarf'} ? (0.7 \leq \text{ObjMassSol} \leq 1.4 ? (0.8 \leq \frac{\text{SourceDistLy}}{\sqrt{\text{ObjMassSol}}} \leq 1.7 ? 2 : 0.5) : 0.3) : 0.1)$, where TOLS (Technological Origin Likelihood Score) is weighted by stellar habitability factors.
[38] Pattern Recognition Confidence (PRC): $\text{PRC} = (\text{RepeatCount} > 1 ? 1 + \log_{10}(\text{RepeatCount}) : 0.5) \times (\text{EntropyVal} < 0.9 ? 1 : 0.3) \times \text{SCR}$, where SCR (Signal Complexity Ratio) provides complexity weighting.
[39] NTM Classification System: Three-tier classification: 'Strong NTM' (BFR < 0.0001 AND FreqDriftHzs < 0.1 AND non-natural modulation), 'Moderate NTM' (BFR < 0.0005 AND FreqDriftHzs < 0.5 AND non-natural modulation), and 'Not NTM' (all other signals).
[40] High-Confidence Technosignature: A Technosignature with $\text{CCS} > 0.9$, $\text{MCS} > 1.5$, and $\text{AIDP} > 0.8$, indicating a signal that meets the basic Technosignature criteria with additional confirmation through modulation complexity and artificial intelligence detection markers.
[41] Habitable Zone Transmission: A signal with $\text{HZSR} > 1.5$ and Technosignature characteristics, originating from a star system with conditions potentially suitable for life, making it a priority candidate for SETI research.
[42] Multi-Channel Communication Protocol: Signal exhibiting Coherent Information Pattern (CIP) characteristics across multiple frequency channels with coordinated timing ($\text{RepeatCount} > 3$, $\text{PeriodSec}$ consistent across observations) and $\text{ECI} > 2.0$, suggesting a designed communication system.
[43] Quantum-Coherent Transmission: Signals with $\text{QuantEffects}$ containing 'Significant' or 'Observed' patterns, exhibiting unusually high information density ($\text{InfoDense} > 1.5$) while maintaining an $\text{ECI} > 2.5$, suggesting advanced transmission technologies beyond conventional radiofrequency methods.
[44] Research Critical Signal: Signals meeting Target of Opportunity (TOO) criteria with additional $\text{PRC} > 0.8$ and $\text{IMDF} < 0.5$, indicating high-quality, minimally distorted signals that show recognizable patterns warranting priority allocation of research resources.
[45] Directed Transmission: Signals with high spatial stability ($\text{SpatStab} = \text{'Moderate'}$), narrow beam characteristics ($\text{PolarMode} = \text{'Linear'}$ with stable $\text{PolarAngleDeg}$), and high $\text{TOLS} > 0.85$, suggesting intentional transmission toward our location.
[46] Signal of Galactic Significance: Signals originating from regions of high $\text{CLSF} (> 2.0)$ that display Technosignature characteristics and have $\text{AIDP} > 0.7$, representing potential evidence of advanced civilizations at galactic-relevant locations.
[47] CCS Approximation: $(1 - \text{FalsePosProb}) \times \text{DecodeConf} \times (\text{SNR} - 0.1 \times |\text{NoiseFloorDbm}| > 0 ? \frac{\text{SNR} - 0.1 \times |\text{NoiseFloorDbm}|}{10} + 0.5 : 0.1)$
[48] Observation-Verified Signal: Signals observed under Optimal Observing Window (OOW) conditions with $\text{OQF} > 0.85$ and $\text{CCS} > 0.8$, indicating high-quality observations with multiple verification methods applied.
[49] Anomalous Quantum Signal: Signals with $\text{QuantEffects}$ indicating anomalous behavior, $\text{AnomScore} > 8$, and unusually high $\text{MCS} (> 2.0)$, suggesting either unknown natural quantum phenomena or extremely advanced transmission technologies beyond current human capabilities.
[50] Analyzable Signals: Signals with SNQI > 0 are considered analyzable.
[51] Bandwidth-to-Frequency Ratio (BFR): $\text{BFR} = \frac{\text{BwHz}}{\text{CenterFreqMhz} \times 1{,}000{,}000}$, used to characterize signal spread relative to its frequency band.
[52] TOLS Category: Categorized as 'Low' if TOLS < 0.25, 'Medium' if TOLS < 0.75, and 'High' otherwise.
[53] High Lunar Interference Events: Events where the calculated LIF is greater than 0.5, indicating strong lunar contamination in the data.
[54] High Confidence Signals: Signals where $\text{CCS} > 0.8$
[55] Equipment Problems: A telescope is considered to have an equipment problem whenever **any** of its key subsystem states are not in their nominal condition: • Equipment status is not “Operational”; • Calibration status is not “Current”; • Cooling-system status is not “Normal”.
